examples: The everity of expressive aphasia varies among patients.
There are disorders of language like aphasia.
And damage to the brain's superior temporal gyrus can lead to Wernicke's aphasia.
Although some people experience a full recovery from aphasia, others only get part of their previous abilities back.
Osofsky-McGonigle had aphasia, a common outcome of stroke in which parts of the brain related to language, including reading, writing and talking, are damaged.
